title: Lovely Smoky Canadian Beef Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful Canadian dish featuring tender beef, smoky seasonings, and a rich broth, perfect for a cold winter's night. This stew is garlicky, chewy, and full of depth.
instructions: Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the beef cubes and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the beef broth, diced tomatoes, carrots, and potatoes, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 1/2 hours, or until the beef is tender.
Remove the pot from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or thyme, if desired.
Refrigerate or freeze any leftovers for up to 3 days.
Reheat the stew over low heat, adding a little water if needed, until warmed through.
